quality.rationale: 通过自旋2场极化旋转对称性与点粒子运动响应的矛盾分析，严谨推导出universal coupling，物理图像深刻且逻辑链条完整

origin.question: Problem 12.16. Show (nonrigorously) that gravity is the only classical, infinite range (massless quanta) pure spin-2 field; i.e. that any other field would couple identically to bulk matter and would thus be indistinguishable from gravity.
origin.solution:
Suppose, to the contrary, that there were another spin-2 field. Since it is massless, and has infinite range, it must admit weak, plane waves as solutions. Also since the field is spin-2 it must couple to a symmetric, traceless "charge" tensor $\mathbf{J}^{\mu \nu}$ . Moreover, for weak fields and slow velocities, "charged" particles which are much smaller than a wavelength must couple predominantly through their $\mathbf{J}^{00}$ (i.e. the "Coulomb" limit).
Consider a plane wave incident on two particles (initially located at the same point) of charge $J^{00} = q_1$ , $J^{00} = q_2$ . For a sinusoidal plane wave of a definite linear polarization the particles must execute linear sinusoidal motion whose amplitude is proportional to their charge and inversely proportional to their mass. Hence, at an instant in time, we can define a polarization vector (analog of the electric field E) for the field
$$
\underline {{{\mathsf {V}}}} \equiv \left(\frac {\mathsf {q}_{1}}{\mathsf {m}_{1}} - \frac {\mathsf {q}_{2}}{\mathsf {m}_{2}}\right)^{- 1} (\underline {{{\mathsf {x}}}}_{1} - \underline {{{\mathsf {x}}}}_{2})
$$
which is independent of the numerical value of $\mathbf{q}_1, \mathbf{q}_2, \mathbf{m}_1, \mathbf{m}_2$ . But this is a contradiction! Since the field has spin-2 structure, a polarization state should be unchanged by $180^\circ$ rotation, but under $180^\circ$ rotation $\underline{\mathbf{V}} \rightarrow -\underline{\mathbf{V}}$ implying $\underline{\mathbf{V}} = 0$ . What is wrong? We assumed that the particles responded differently to the field. We now see that $\underline{\mathbf{x}}_1 = \underline{\mathbf{x}}_2$ for all charges and masses is the only possibility. This is equivalent to saying that all particles have a fixed charge-to-mass ratio: $\mathbf{q} / \mathbf{m} = \text{constant}$ . By choice of units we can make $\mathbf{q} = \mathbf{m}$ . Hence, in the weak field, plane-wave limit $\mathbf{J}^{00} = \mathbf{T}^{00}$ , the stress energy. But by Lorentz invariance we must have $\mathbf{J}^{\mu \nu} = \mathbf{T}^{\mu \nu}$ (e.g. we cannot introduce $\mathbf{T}_{\mu}^{\mu}$ since this is not spin-2).
We have shown that our "arbitrary" spin-2 field is coupled to $\mathbf{T}^{\mu \nu}$ . Hence, it is just a piece of gravity, i.e. whatever we measure experimentally as gravity. (No claim that it is general relativity is made.) R. P. Feynman has pointed out that the assumption of a "point" particle, with no intrinsic polarization vector, has entered crucially. Thus it is possible to have the field couple to individual particles with spin; but for unpolarized bulk matter, there can be no net force.
